article_title: Curiosity's First TMAH Wet Chemistry Run on Mars Yields More Than 20 Organic Molecules, Seven Never Detected Before on the Planet

content_preview.title: Curiosity's First TMAH Wet Chemistry Run on Mars Yields More Than 20 Organic Molecules, Seven Never Detected Before on the Planet
content_preview.summary: A Nature Communications paper published April 21 reports that the first off-Earth TMAH thermochemolysis experiment, performed on a clay-rich sample in Gale crater, expanded Mars's known organic inventory and detected a candidate nitrogen heterocycle for the first time on the surface.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

NASA's Curiosity rover has produced the most diverse inventory of organic molecules ever measured on Mars, after scientists ran the rover's onboard wet-chemistry experiment for the first time on a sample drilled from clay-bearing rocks on Mount Sharp. According to a [NASA announcement](https://www.nasa.gov/missions/mars-science-laboratory/curiosity-rover/nasas-curiosity-finds-organic-molecules-never-seen-before-on-mars/) on April 21, 2026, the analysis identified 21 carbon-containin...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Round-2 rewrite cleanly addresses all 8 round-1 findings. Word count 909 (in News range). Structure intact, tone unchanged.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 7 local snapshots (Nature paper added as 7th source). Independent re-verification confirms: 'thermochemolysis' verbatim in Nature paper (5+ occurrences); the 4 previously-unsourced molecule names (methylnaphthalene, dihydronaphthalene, trimethylbenzene, tetramethylbenzene) all verbatim in Nature paper; Knockfarrill Hill / Glen Torridon / ~3.5 Gyr verbatim in Nature paper; Williams 'professor of geological sciences' verbatim in phys.org; Glen Torridon / clay framing verified across phys.org and CNN; JPL image caption Oct. 25 2020 drill-site selfie verified.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All round-1 attribution issues cleanly resolved. One minor residual concern: the article says SAM routes gases through 'gas chromatograph and quadrupole mass spectrometer' attributed to the Nature paper, but the paper's main framing is 'GC-MS' / 'gas chromatography-mass spectrometry'. The 'QMS' abbreviation appears once buried in methods. The phrase 'quadrupole mass spectrometer' is technically correct (SAM does have a quadrupole MS, documented in NASA's SAM instrument literature) but is not verbatim in this Nature paper. Acceptable as written but could be tightened to 'gas chromatograph–mass spectrometer' or 'GC-MS' to match the paper's preferred language.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and scientific. The terminology choice between 'mudstone' (popular press) and 'sandstone' (Nature paper) is handled honestly — bot uses 'clay-bearing rocks' in the Overview and 'clay-rich mudstone' attributed to phys.org/CNN, separating popular and technical framings.
concerns: Minor: 'quadrupole mass spectrometer' is technically correct but not verbatim in the cited Nature paper. The paper consistently uses 'GC-MS' / 'gas chromatography-mass spectrometry' framing. Not a blocker.
recommendations: Optional: tighten 'gas chromatograph and quadrupole mass spectrometer' to 'gas chromatograph–mass spectrometer (GC-MS)' to match the paper's preferred language.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round-2 rewrite cleanly resolves all 8 attribution issues from round 1. The Nature paper addition is the right fix and the bot used it well — most technical specifics are now sourced to the paper rather than to general-press outlets that don't have them. One minor residual quibble on the GC/QMS terminology but not a blocker. APPROVE — ready to merge.
round_1_findings_resolved: Glen Torridon / Gale crater / mudstone — re-attributed to phys.org + CNN (NASA citation now uses only 'Mount Sharp' / 'clay-bearing rocks').
'Thermochemolysis' — re-attributed to Nature paper (verbatim in title and body).
Hydrolyzes/methylates mechanism — replaced with 'breaks apart larger organic molecules' attributed to Nature, plus plain-language JPL paraphrase.
GC + quadrupole MS — re-attributed to Nature paper (with minor terminology nuance noted above).
4 unnamed molecule names — re-attributed to Nature paper (all verbatim).
Williams 'professor of geological sciences' — re-attributed to phys.org (verbatim).
October 2020 drill date — re-attributed to JPL image caption (Oct. 25 2020).
3.5-billion-year mudstone age — re-attributed to Nature paper (verbatim, 'Knockfarrill Hill member of Glen Torridon, ~3.5 Gyr').
